title: Moovar Koil
tagline: Moovar Koil — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Moovar Koil or "The Three temples" is a Hindu temple complex situated in the village of Kodumbalur, 36 kilometres from Pudukkottai in Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Moovar Koil or "The Three temples" is a Hindu temple complex situated in the village of Kodumbalur, 36 kilometres from Pudukkottai in Tamil Nadu, India. These temples were constructed by the Chola feudatory and Irukkuvel chieftain Boothi Vikramakesari as per the inscription. Only two of the three temples have managed to survive. The place was ruled by Irukkuvel chieftains.

topPlaces.name: Kodumbalur
topPlaces.description: Kodumbalur is a village in Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is the site of the Moovar Koil temple complex.

topPlaces.name: Thennambadi
topPlaces.description: Thennambadi, or Thennambady is a small village in Tamil Nadu, India located 6 km From Viralimalai. Thennambadi is one of the village in Viralimalai Taluk in Pudukkottai District in Tamil Nadu State.

topPlaces.name: Thirukattupalli
topPlaces.description: Thirukattupalli (Ancient name: Melaithirukattupalli) is a panchayat town near Thiruvaiyaru in Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The economy of this town is based on the agricultural lands of the surrounding villages.

topPlaces.name: Viralimalai
topPlaces.description: Viralimalai is a developing town and Suburb of city of Tiruchirappalli, located in Pudukkottai District, 28 km from Tiruchirappalli in Tamil Nadu, India. The famous Lord Shanmuganathar temple is situated on the top of the granite hill at Viralimalai.

topPlaces.name: Viralimalai Bird Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Viralimalai Bird Sanctuary is a protected area and bird sanctuary located in Tiruchirappalli district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The sanctuary was established to protect the Indian peafowl, the national bird of India.

topPlaces.name: Ennai
topPlaces.description: Ennai is a village in the Annavasal revenue block of Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

items.note: Kodumbalur is a village in Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is the site of the Moovar Koil temple complex.
Thennambadi, or Thennambady is a small village in Tamil Nadu, India located 6 km From Viralimalai. Thennambadi is one of the village in Viralimalai Taluk in Pudukkottai District in Tamil Nadu State.

Thirukattupalli (Ancient name: Melaithirukattupalli) is a panchayat town near Thiruvaiyaru in Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The economy of this town is based on the agricultural lands of the surrounding villages.

Viralimalai is a developing town and Suburb of city of Tiruchirappalli, located in Pudukkottai District, 28 km from Tiruchirappalli in Tamil Nadu, India. The famous Lord Shanmuganathar temple is situated on the top of the granite hill at Viralimalai.

Viralimalai Bird Sanctuary is a protected area and bird sanctuary located in Tiruchirappalli district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The sanctuary was established to protect the Indian peafowl, the national bird of India.
Ennai is a village in the Annavasal revenue block of Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Moovar Koil?
faq.a: The best time to visit Moovar Koil is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Moovar Koil?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (32 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (34 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Moovar Koil?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Moovar Koil at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Moovar Koil?
faq.a: Stays in Moovar Koil start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Moovar Koil?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kodumbalur, Thennambadi, Thirukattupalli, Viralimalai, Viralimalai Bird Sanctuary.
